channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 'Even Marco Rubio was...': Any arrest may lead to work permit denial in the US under new proposal; Immigration lawyers urge caution
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Melinda French Gates: "When a woman can step into her full power, it’s better for…" - a powerful reminder every woman needs to hear today
item.description: When women reach their full potential, their impact extends far beyond themselves. Empowering women leads to better health and education for their children. Financial independence for women improves household stability and community well-being. Greater female participation in leadership shapes policies for societal development. This concept highlights how individual opportunity translates into collective benefit.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists built a reusable magnetic material that removed more than 95% of microplastics from water
item.description: Innovative Australian researchers have developed a groundbreaking reusable magnetic material that effectively eliminates over ninety-five percent of microplastics from water. This remarkable technology also exhibits potential in removing harmful PFAS chemicals. The magnetic powder quickly captures tiny plastic particles and heavy metals, enabling easy recovery with magnets after use, thereby significantly cutting down on waste and operational costs. This advancement offers a promising solution for global water treatment facilities.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Henry Kissinger: 'Don't be too ambitious. Do the most important thing you can think of doing every year and then your career will take care of itself' and a masterstroke career advice
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: These rare 'black roses' grow naturally in only one region of Turkey, and this natural wonder is known as 'Louis XIV
item.description: Turkey's black rose is a rare flower with a unique colour transformation. This remarkable bloom appears almost black before turning deep crimson. Its extraordinary colour is a result of local soil and climate conditions. Scientists are now working to conserve this flower and its natural habitat.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump says he is 'close' to decision on 'massive attack' on Iran, bigger than Epic Fury
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A Florida community has America’s largest man-made lagoon, a 15-acre turquoise pool with...
item.description: A housing community in Florida boasts access to the largest artificial swimming pool in the United States, known as the impressive 15-acre Crystal Lagoon. This amenity is driving a surge in real estate demand as buyers opt for inland properties, seeking tropical benefits and lower storm risks. The developer is set for further growth by introducing new projects with similar attractions, offering a coastal lifestyle far from the sea.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistani man to be deported out of US for immigration fraud; used three names to become a citizen in 2009
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists tested pet-store 'superworms' on animal specimens and found the beetle...
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 'Committed jihadist': UK-born Pakistani man jailed over military base terror plot
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: America’s deepest man-made lakes were created by flooding ancient river valleys behind massive dams; here’s the list
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: West Virginia flash floods: 2 missing, 500 National Guard troops deployed amid torrential rain
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How ancient humans made their beds: 200,000-year-old grass reveals a prehistoric cleaning routine
item.description: Ancient grass bedding found in a South African cave shows early humans cared for sleeping areas. These hunter-gatherers arranged plant materials and used fire to maintain their resting places. The discovery at Border Cave offers a rare glimpse into prehistoric daily habits. Evidence suggests humans deliberately shaped their sleeping spaces much earlier than previously thought. This finding supports growing evidence of meticulous environmental management by prehistoric communities.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Bangladesh plans to plant 250 million trees in five years; conservationists warn the wrong trees could damage entire ecosystems
item.description: Bangladesh plans to plant 250 million trees over five years. This initiative aims to boost green cover and meet climate goals. The government is coordinating efforts across multiple ministries and agencies. Careful species selection and post-planting care are crucial for success. The project's impact depends on balancing science with community needs.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pennsylvania families sell 1,700 acres for $586M; couple gets $22M for 89-acre hog farm
item.description: The AI infrastructure race has turned a quiet Pennsylvania farming community into one of the country's hottest real estate markets. As 96 families sold 1,700 acres for a combined $586 million to a Blackstone-backed data center developer, David and Marilee Kiliti received over $22 million for their 89-acre hog farm. The unexpected windfall has allowed the family to clear debts, make new investments and completely transform their future.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Can astronauts cry in space?
item.description: Astronauts experience tears differently in space due to the absence of gravity. Liquid tears do not fall but instead form blobs around the eyes. Canadian astronaut Chris Hadfield demonstrated this phenomenon with water. NASA astronaut Clayton Anderson also confirmed crying occurs without tears falling. This scientific reality impacts cinematic portrayals of emotional moments in space.
item.dc:creator: Silpa Rajan

item.title: Wife, daughter of Bangladeshi politician drown rescuing child at UK beach
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: FBI's wanted fraudster arrested overseas; Khalid Ahmed Satary was on the run after $547 million Medicare fraud
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Empty seashells became the ocean’s first ready-made homes 470 million years ago
item.description: Approximately 470 million years ago, ancient mollusc shells started to serve as habitats for small sea creatures, initiating a profound ecological transformation across global ocean ecosystems. These empty shells offered safe havens and surfaces for tiny marine animals to cling to, with their shapes playing crucial roles in determining the size and variety of these diverse communities, an ancient behavior that laid the groundwork for today's oceanic life.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Six fluffy barn owlets were found nesting on a farm near Peterborough, bringing good news after the farm faced six suspected arson attacks this summer
item.description: A surprise brood of six barn owlets has brought welcome cheer to a Cambridgeshire farm. These fluffy youngsters were discovered and ringed as part of local conservation efforts. The farm owner expressed relief after a difficult summer marked by suspected arson attacks. Volunteer Simon Dudhill called finding six owlets exceptionally good for this year. These birds are a reminder of how local action helps wildlife recover.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
